title: Reference unbounded or independently-updated data
description: Store large, growing, or shared child sets in their own collection and link by id to avoid runaway document growth.
body: Use when a related set is unbounded, grows over time, or is shared across many parents. Do not embed an ever-growing array (events, comments, log lines) into a parent document; it walks toward the 16MB limit and rewrites the whole document on every push. Actions: put the child set in its own collection keyed by the parent id, index that key, and join with $lookup only when a combined view is genuinely needed. Validation signals include parent documents staying small and stable in size and child inserts not rewriting the parent. Risk boundary: a $lookup across large unindexed collections is effectively a nested loop join, so confirm the foreign key is indexed before relying on it.
